<h1>Real-time/Field-Based Research Project Report On</h1><p><strong>&lt;Title of the Project&gt;</strong></p><p>A dissertation submitted to the Jawaharlal Nehru Technological University, Hyderabad in partial fulfillment of the requirement for the award of a degree of</p><h2>BACHELOR OF TECHNOLOGY IN</h2><p><strong>COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING</strong></p><p>Submitted by</p><p><strong>&lt;Student Name&gt; (H.T.Number)</strong></p><p><strong>&lt;Student Name&gt; (H.T.Number)</strong></p><p><strong>&lt;Student Name&gt; (H.T.Number)</strong></p><p><strong><img alt="A logo of a college of engineering  Description automatically generated" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," /></strong></p><p>Department of Computer Science and Engineering</p><p>CVR COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING</p><p>(An UGC Autonomous Institution, Affiliated to JNTUH, Accredited by NBA, and NAAC) Vastunagar, Mangalpalli (V), Ibrahimpatnam (M), Ranga Reddy (Dist.) - 501510,</p><p>Telangana State.</p><p><strong>2025-26</strong></p><h1><img alt="A logo of a college of engineering  Description automatically generated" src="data:image/jpeg;base64," />CVR COLLEGE OF ENGINEERING</h1><p><em>(</em>An UGC Autonomous Institution, Affiliated to JNTUH, Accredited by NBA, and NAAC)</p><p>Vastunagar, Mangalpalli (V), Ibrahimpatnam (M), Ranga Reddy (Dist.) - 501510, Telangana State.</p><h2>DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING</h2><p><strong>CERTIFICATE</strong></p><p>This is to certify that the project work entitled <strong>“&lt;Title of the Project&gt;” </strong>is being submitted by &lt;Names of Student (Hall Ticket Numbers)&gt; in partial fulfillment of the requirement for the award of the degree of <strong>Bachelor of Technology </strong>in <strong>Computer Science and Engineering, </strong>during the academic year 2025-2026.</p><p><strong>Professor and Head, CSE (Dr. A. Vani Vathsala)</strong></p><p><strong> Supervisor                                    Professor-in-charge RFP	      	 </strong></p><p><strong>   </strong></p><p><strong>DECLARATION</strong></p><p>I hereby declare that this project report titled <strong>“&lt;Title of the Project&gt;” </strong>submitted to the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, CVR College of Engineering, is a record of original work done by me. The information and data given in the report is authentic to the best of my knowledge. This Real Time/Field-Based Research Project report is not submitted to any other university or institution for the award of any degree or diploma or published at any time before.</p><p><strong>&lt; Student Name- Hall Ticket Number &gt;</strong></p><p><strong>&lt; Student Name- Hall Ticket Number &gt;</strong></p><p><strong>&lt; Student Name- Hall Ticket Number &gt;</strong></p><p>Date: Place:</p><p><strong>General Guidelines for Real-Time/Field-Based Research Project (RFP) Final Report Preparation</strong></p><ol><li>All the students should refer to the project circulars/guidelines and adhere to the deadlines/ timelines of various activities. These dates are subject to minor changes, and proximity to the dates may be assumed for all the activities or events during project work. In case of any changes will be communicated by the RFP Section Coordinator, and they should be updated in the Project work.</li><li>Ensure that suggestions/corrections discussed during the internal evaluations are incorporated in the final report.</li><li>Formats of the project title page and certificates given in this handout should be strictly adhered to.</li><li>Submit each of the following at the time of final report submission:<ol><li>Four Spiral-bound copies of your Real-Time/Field-Based Research Project (One for the library and one per student in the group).</li><li>Soft copy of the Project's final document (PDF format).</li><li>Executable code Zip Folder (Zip Folder: Section_BatchNo_Title).</li></ol></li></ol><p>Example: Zip file name should be: CSE_E_01_Myproject</p><ol><li>The sequence of the project document pages should be as follows:<ol><li>Cover Page</li><li>Project Title Page</li><li>Certificate from the Professor in charge RFP.</li><li>Declaration</li><li>Acknowledgement</li><li>Abstract</li><li>Table of content</li><li>List of tables (if any)</li><li>List of figures (if any)</li><li>Abbreviations, symbols, and nomenclature (if any)</li><li>Chapters</li></ol></li></ol><p>1. Introduction 2.Literature Survey 3.Requirement Analysis 4.Design</p><ul><li><ul><li><ol><li>Implementation and Testing</li><li>Conclusion</li></ol></li></ul><ol><li>Appendices</li><li>References</li><li>List of papers published, based on the report (if any)</li></ol></li></ul><p><strong>Follow these formats:</strong></p><h2>REFERENCES</h2><p><strong>Articles:</strong></p><ol><li>Hwanam Kim, Byungchul Choi,” Effect of ethanol diesel blend fuels on emission and particle size distribution in a common-rail direct injection diesel engine with warm-up catalytic converter”, Renewable Energy, Vol. 33, Issue 10, pp. 2222-2228, 2008.</li></ol><p><strong>Books:</strong></p><ol><li>Holt, D.H. 1997. Management principles and practices. Sydney: Prentice-Hall.</li></ol><p><strong>Websites:</strong></p><ol><li>https://<a href="http://www.class2go.uwa.edu.au/DevSocImag/Summer2013/v">www.class2go.uwa.edu.au/DevSocImag/Summer2013/v</a>ideos.dvm. [30 May</li></ol><p>2013].</p><p><strong>Project Report format:</strong></p><table><tr><td><p>Paper quality</p></td><td><p>Printed on white bond paper, weight 70 GSM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Paper Size</p></td><td><p>The standard A4</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Manuscript Preparation</p></td><td><p>MS Word or LaTeX</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Language</p></td><td><p>Indian English.</p><p>Check for consistent spelling of names, terms, and abbreviations, including tables and figures captions.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Size of Project Report</p></td><td><p>The number of pages of the Report should be preferably between 25-50 pages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Font, Size, Line spacing</p></td><td><p>Times New Roman of 12 pts with1.5 line spacing.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Margins</p></td><td><p>A margin of 3.75cm (1½ inch) is to be given on the binding edge</p><p>while on the other side it is to be 2.5cm(1inch).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Binding</p></td><td><p>The report submitted for Final evaluation must be Spiral bind and printed on one side.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cover Page Color</p></td><td><p>Cover Page-Transparent White Color</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Justification</p></td><td><ul><li>The text should be fully justified.</li><li>The text in the tables should be left justified.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pagination</p></td><td><ul><li>The page numbering, starting from acknowledgements and till the beginning of the introductory chapter, should be printed in small Roman numbers, i.e,i, ii, iii, iv...</li><li>The page number of the first page of each chapter should not be printed (but must be accounted for).</li><li>All page numbers from the Introduction chapter page and each chapter should be printed using Arabic numerals, i.e.1, 2, 3, 4, 5...</li><li>All printed page numbers should be located at the bottom center of the page.</li></ul></td></tr></table><table><tr><td><p>Chapters</p></td><td><p>Each chapter shall begin on a New page.</p><p>1. Introduction 2.Literature Survey 3.Requirement Analysis 4.Design</p><p>5.Implementation and Testing 6.Conclusion</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sections and Sub sections</p></td><td><p>Each topic Title in a chapter should be properly numbered for example: 2.1, 2.2. etc.,(Bold and Upper case) where, the first digit represents the Chapter number, and second digit, the topic Title number.</p><p>Sub-topic titles, if any, may be indicated as 1.1.1, 1.1.2, etc. (Bold and title case) i.e. the first digit representing the chapter, the second</p><p>representing the topic title and the third representing the sub-topic title.</p></td></tr></table><p><strong>TABLE OF CONTENTS</strong></p><table><tr><td colspan="3"></td><td><p>Page
